title: Cascaded auxiliary head for laps-to-next-stop
summary: Recent lap-level F1 work reports a cascaded multi-task setup where next-lap stop classification is complemented by a horizon-to-stop regression head.
rationale: Recent lap-level F1 work reports a cascaded multi-task setup where next-lap stop classification is complemented by a horizon-to-stop regression head. This is a stronger, more specific version of generic horizon-aware supervision because the auxiliary prediction itself becomes a learned summary of stop imminence.
implementation_hint: Create a training-only label for laps_until_next_pit within each driver-race sequence. Train an auxiliary model for that target, generate strict out-of-fold predictions for train and one-pass predictions for test, then feed predicted horizon, log-horizon, and uncertainty/error bins into the main ROC-AUC model.
expected_effect: Improves ranking around 2-6 laps before a stop, where binary labels alone are sparse and noisy.
risk: If in-fold auxiliary predictions are reused on train, leakage is severe; only OOF auxiliary predictions should enter the final classifier.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
